summary:
The patient likely has Vitamin K deficiency due to abnormal prothrombin time (PT) and activated partial thromboplastin time (APTT), while maintaining normal levels of fibrinogen and platelets. Evidence supports this by showing how deficiencies affect specific components involved only when vitamin K plays an essential role during blood clot formation processes. To confirm, treatment response should be monitored closely after administering vitamin K therapy if not already done so clinically. ```plaintext
Key Points Summary:

- Diagnosis favored: Vitamin K Deficiency because PT/INR and APTT are elevated but fibrinogen and platelets are within normal range.
- Confirmatory test suggested: Rapid normalization post-vitamin K injection or close monitoring before finalizing the diagnosis.
```
